content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901          322-6441           322-6444

April 12, 1983


Don White
Reclamation District, No. 1001
1959 Cornelius Avenue
Rio Oso, CA  95764

Re: Advice No. A-83-095

Dear Mr. White:

     Thank you for your request for advice.  Your question was
whether a member of the Board of Trustees of Reclamation
District, No. 1001, may purchase a short term note from the
District.  If he purchases the note, the board member will
receive interest on the loan and eventual repayment of it.

     Government Code Section 87100 1/ states that no official at
any level of local government shall make or participate in the
making of a governmental decision in which he knows or has
reason to know he has a financial interest.  An official has a
"financial interest" in a decision within the meaning of Section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect on:

                        . . .

     (c)  Any source of income . . . aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to . . . the public official within 12 months prior to
the time when the decision is made.

               (Section 87103(c).)

_________________________

     1/  All statutory references are to the Government
Code unless otherwise noted.

`

Don White
April 12, 1983
Page 2


     In the present case, the interest and repayment
received on the note will not constitute income to the
Reclamation District will not become a source of income to
the board member because the Act's definition of "income"
excludes "payments received under any ... debt instrument
issued by any government ... agency." (Section
82030(b)(4).) Thus, the board member may purchase the note
without subjecting himself to the Act's disqualification
provisions.

     Please note that my advice is limited to the provisions
of the Political Reform Act and does not include the
possible application of other provisions, such as Section
1090. If I can be any further help to you, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   Janis Shank McLean
                                   Janis Shank McLean
                                   Counsel
                                   Legal Division

JSM:pln

sections.question: Whether a member of the Board of Trustees of Reclamation District, No. 1001, may purchase a short term note from the District. If he purchases the note, the board member will receive interest on the loan and eventual repayment of it.
sections.conclusion: The interest and repayment received on the note will not constitute income and the Reclamation District will not become a source of income to the board member because the Act's definition of "income" excludes "payments received under any ... debt instrument issued by any government ... agency." Thus, the board member may purchase the note without subjecting himself to the Act's disqualification provisions.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a board member of Reclamation District No. 1001 purchase a short-term note from the district that would provide interest income and repayment without violating conflict of interest provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The board member may purchase the short-term note because payments received under debt instruments issued by government agencies are excluded from the definition of "income" under the Political Reform Act, thus avoiding disqualification under Section 87100.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Advice number A-83-095 dated April 12, 1983. FPPC counsel Janis Shank McLean notes that advice is limited to Political Reform Act provisions and does not address Section 1090 or other statutes.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a member of the Board of Trustees of Reclamation District, No. 1001, may purchase a short term note from the District. If he purchases the note, the board member will receive interest on the loan and eventual repayment of it.

CONCLUSION: The interest and repayment received on the note will not constitute income and the Reclamation District will not become a source of income to the board member because the Act's definition of "income" excludes "payments received under any ... debt instrument issued by any government ... agency." Thus, the board member may purchase the note without subjecting himself to the Act's disqualification provisions.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that a board member of Reclamation District No. 1001 may purchase a short-term note from the district without violating conflict of interest rules because debt instrument payments from government agencies are excluded from income under the Political Reform Act. The advice is limited to the Political Reform Act and does not address other potential statutory provisions.
